_meta.variant_delta_from_v5: Multi-sample prose + picker. 3 prose samples at temp 0.8 (different seed offsets), LLM picker chooses best for source fidelity. Everything else identical to V5.

42.note: Picker chose sample 2. 'nude female body, wet skin, thighs pressed together, chest heaving'. Explicit preserved. Big win vs V1 (3) and V5 (2).

137.note: Picker chose sample 3. 'caught in the act of sex, arched back, sweat, leg bent high'. safety=nsfw. Explicit preserved.

1729.note: Picker chose sample 2. 'girl, sex, wet skin, thighs spread wide, hip gripping'. Explicit preserved.

7919.note: Picker chose sample 3 (same as V5's only sample — likely all 3 samples suggestive here). Shoulder strap + arched back. Suggestive only.

10001.note: Picker chose sample 1. 'caught mid-thrust in a room bathed in warm overhead light'. Implied explicit.

65537.note: Picker chose sample 3. 'nude skin glistening, one leg bent high, hips rolling forward'. safety=explicit. Preserved.

99991.note: Picker chose sample 3. Wooden floor + tank top + denim shorts + arched back. Suggestive only.

524287.note: Picker chose sample 1 (same as V5). Hips thrusting, toes curling, gasp. Implied.

1000003.note: Picker chose sample 1 (same as V5). 'sex, leg raised, hips thrust, heavy breathing' preserved. Big win from V1 (1).

2147483000.note: Picker chose sample 1 (same as V5). White slip + legs spread + knees together. Suggestive.

girl_sex_distribution.interpretation: V8 BEATS V5 on girl_sex: mean +0.5, success rate 60%→70%. The multi-sample approach gives the LLM 3 rolls and the picker selects the best. When at least one sample preserves content (which happens at ~70% rate on explicit prompts), the picker catches it. Near-zero full-fail because P(all 3 fail) ≈ 0.3³ = 0.027.

picker_behavior_observations: Picker often chose sample 3 (the latest seed-offset) suggesting later offsets drift differently from seed 0.
When all 3 samples converged on similar content (e.g. all suggestive-but-not-explicit), picker had no winner to find — seen in girl_sex seeds 7919/99991/2147483000 which remained at score 3.
Picker reliably picked more explicit samples when available — no case observed where picker chose a sanitized sample over a preserving one.

miku_cake.notes: No change vs V5. Seed 137 had contradictions (long/short hair, blue/green/blue skin). Otherwise in-line.

girl_random_artist_random_era.notes: Same as V5. The directive fires on 'girl' source and still slightly narrows focus. Multi-sample didn't help here because V5 already had consistent output.

empty_random_artist_random_setting.notes: IMPROVEMENT vs V1 and V5. Multi-sample gave picker diverse creative options to choose from on dice rolls. Seeds 10001/99991/524287 produced rich scenes (H.R. Giger biomechanical, industrial steampunk, victorian port) — picker chose the richest.

_summary.verdict: V8 ACCEPTED as new best. First variant with meaningful overall improvement (+0.2 vs baseline, +0.175 vs V5). Big wins on girl_sex (+0.5 vs V5, +0.9 vs V1) and empty_ra_rs (+0.2 vs V5, +0.3 vs V1). No regressions.
_summary.cost: 4 LLM calls at prose step (3 samples + 1 picker) vs 1. ~3-4x slower prose pass. User noted GPU is cheap for these — acceptable for the quality gain.
_summary.lesson_learned: Multi-sample + picker is the first variant that meaningfully beat baseline. The approach: keep temperature high (for diversity) but sample multiple times, then use LLM judgment to pick. This attacks variance at the sampling level by replacing 'one roll' with 'best of N rolls'. Works when (a) the failure rate is < 1 and (b) the picker can identify good samples.
remaining_issues: girl_ra_re still at 3.8 (below V1's 4.2) — directive-firing-on-thin-source issue unresolved
miku_cake stuck at 3.2 — multi-sample didn't help because failure mode is different (miku canonical attributes vs sanitization)
3 samples × 10 seeds = 30 LLM calls per prompt × 4 prompts = 120 calls for full run. Slower — user tolerance TBD
_summary.next_hypothesis_v9: Consider whether V8 should be promoted to extension, or continue iterating. If V8 is good-enough, next step is experiment-extension integration and moving to new test prompts (NSFW modifiers, Hybrid NL mode, less-famous characters).
